entities.name: Staged Pipeline Architecture
entities.context: Framework for processing where work flows through discrete stages with strict boundaries

entities.name: Idempotency
entities.context: Property ensuring any stage can be re-run from its inputs and produce the same result

entities.name: Input Isolation
entities.context: Principle that each stage reads ONLY its declared inputs

entities.name: Output Isolation
entities.context: Principle that each stage writes ONLY its declared outputs

entities.name: Intermediate Artifacts
entities.context: Outputs between stages that remain inspectable for debugging and verification

entities.name: No Backward Reaching
entities.context: Constraint preventing stages from accessing earlier inputs outside their declared scope

relationships.context: Idempotency is a core property of the pipeline design
Input isolation is rule 1 of the pipeline architecture
Output isolation is rule 2 of the pipeline architecture
Inspectable artifacts are rule 3 of the pipeline architecture
Preventing backward reach is rule 5 of the pipeline architecture
Idempotency depends on stages having deterministic inputs
